metadata.title:
PASSENGERS RUSH FROM


BURNING CAR INTO SNOW

pages.raw_text:
awoke under the impression that
the bank deposit guarantee law
had been passed. A lady in the car
decamped in such haste that she
left her hustle behind to perish. An
unknown hero rescued it, amid
thunders of applause.


1t was stated that this particular
car had already been reported
twice for poor gas pressure, and to
overcome this difficulty it was
claimed that the safety valve, had
been screwed down. The tank fail
ed to stand the additional strain,
and the explosion and fire was the
result. Had the train been 20 min
utes. further east. 1t would have
been in the big tunnel at the top
Of the Cascades, In which case the
FIRE would likely have resulted in
loss of life, or at least serious in
jury to the passengers.


Much Indignation was expressed
among the passengers that the cars
are not provided with electric
lights, which minimize the danger
of fire. 1t Is claimed that the pres
ent gas system is cheaper to ham
die than the electricity, and for this
reason the railroad has failed to
put in the latter system. However,
the Great Northern alone has lost
many hundreds of thousands Of
dollars worth of equipment In the
past year by fire, which has result
ed in almost every instance from
the gas tanks.
